perturbation.original_text: 3. Recipient Obligations . The Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care; not use the Confidential Information, or permit it to be accessed or used, for any purpose other than the Purpose, including without limitation, to reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or design around confidenti al intellectual property; not disclose any such Confidential Information to any person or entity, except to the Recipient's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose and are informed of the obligations hereunder and agree to abide by the same. Recipient will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information or other breaches of this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Recipient Obligations . The Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information only when expressly requested by the Disclosing Party; not use the Confidential Information, or permit it to be accessed or used, for any purpose other than the Purpose, including without limitation, to reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or design around confidenti al intellectual property; not disclose any such Confidential Information to any person or entity, except to the Recipient's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose and are informed of the obligations hereunder and agree to abide by the same. Recipient will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information or other breaches of this Agreement .
perturbation.explanation: The modification restricts the obligation to protect Confidential Information to instances when expressly requested by the disclosing party. This directly contradicts the rest of Section 3, which outlines general duties regardless of whether the Disclosing party makes a request, creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Recipient Obligations . The Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information only when expressly requested by the Disclosing Party; not use the Confidential Information, or permit it to be accessed or used, for any purpose other than the Purpose, including without limitation, to reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or design around confidenti al intellectual property; not disclose any such Confidential Information to any person or entity, except to the Recipient's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose and are informed of the obligations hereunder and agree to abide by the same. Recipient will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information or other breaches of this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition that the Recipient only needs to protect Confidential Information when 'expressly requested by the Disclosing Party'. This directly contradicts the subsequent clauses in the same section (Section 3), which impose general obligations on the Recipient to protect Confidential Information regardless of whether the Disclosing Party makes a request. This creates a direct conflict and ambiguity as to when the Recipient is obligated to protect the information, breaking the consistency of the section and fitting the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return or Destruction of Confidential Information. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or at the Disclosing Party's request at any time during the term of this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives shall promptly return to the Disclosing Party all copies, whether in written, electronic or other form or media, of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, or destroy all such copies and confirm the same in writing to the Disclosing Party ; provid ed, that the Recipient and its Representatives may retain such Confidential Information as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return or Destruction of Confidential Information. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, or at the Disclosing Party's request at any time during the term of this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives may, at their sole discretion, return to the Disclosing Party all copies, whether in written, electronic or other form or media, of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, or destroy all such copies and confirm the same in writing to the Disclosing Party ; provid ed, that the Recipient and its Representatives may retain such Confidential Information as is necessary to enable it to comply with its reasonable document retention policies.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall promptly return' to 'may, at their sole discretion, return' fundamentally changes the mandatory nature of returning confidential information. This now contradicts section 3 (Recipient Obligations) which dictates Recipient must protect and safeguard confidentiality, even after the agreement terms end.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Recipient Obligations . The Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care; not use the Confidential Information, or permit it to be accessed or used, for any purpose other than the Purpose, including without limitation, to reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or design around confidenti al intellectual property; not disclose any such Confidential Information to any person or entity, except to the Recipient's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose and are informed of the obligations hereunder and agree to abide by the same. Recipient will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information or other breaches of this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'shall promptly return' with 'may, at their sole discretion, return', altering the mandatory nature of returning confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, section 3 (Recipient Obligations), which mandates the Recipient to protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information. By making the return of confidential information discretionary, the modified text conflicts with the obligation to protect confidentiality, even after the agreement ends, as outlined in section 3.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Remedies . The Recipient acknowledges and agrees t hat money damages might not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives. Therefore, in addition to all other remedies available at law, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive and other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach, and the Recipient hereby waives any requirement for the securing or posting of any bond or the showing of actual monetary damages in connec tion with such claim.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Remedies . The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that money damages might not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives. Therefore, in addition to all other remedies available at law, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive and other equitable relief only if monetary damages are proven insufficient, as determined by a court of law, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach, and the Recipient hereby waives any requirement for the securing or posting of any bond or the showing of actual monetary damages in connec tion with such claim.
perturbation.explanation: The added text restricts the Disclosing Party's right to seek equitable relief (specific performance, injunction) until a court determines that monetary damages are insufficient. The original clause stated Disclosing party 'shall be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive and other equitable relief as a remedy'. The added text conflicts with the original's intention, by creating a condition precedent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Remedies . The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that money damages might not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives. Therefore, in addition to all other remedies available at law, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive and other equitable relief only if monetary damages are proven insufficient, as determined by a court of law, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach, and the Recipient hereby waives any requirement for the securing or posting of any bond or the showing of actual monetary damages in connec tion with such claim.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition precedent for seeking equitable relief, requiring a court determination of monetary damages insufficiency. This directly contradicts the original text's grant of entitlement to seek such relief without this condition, creating an in-text contradiction. The changed text restricts the Disclosing Party's rights, which is a clear conflict.
